name: Piney Woods Hush Puppies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the yellow corn meal, baking powder, soda, salt, sugar, and flour.
2. In another bowl, beat the egg and then add buttermilk until combined with the dry ingredients. The batter should hold its shape when picked up in a spoon.
3. If the batter is too soft, add more cornmeal.
4. Heat cooking oil to 350°F (175°C).
5. Drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot oil and fry for about 1 1/2 minutes on each side until golden brown.
6. Allow oil to cool slightly before frying additional batches. Hush puppies are delicious when freshly cooked and hot, but they also freeze well. To reheat frozen hush puppies, place them in a preheated oven at 250°F (121°C) for about 5 minutes or until very hot and crisp.
Makes approximately 48 hush puppies.
Serving suggestions: Especially good served with fried catfish, trout, shrimp, oysters, chicken, or chicken-fried steak.
